_framing: Defensive security education for blue-team awareness content. Blue-team only. These topics describe real attack patterns solely to teach detection, mitigation, and defense to a general audience — never to provide offensive tooling or operational how-to.
voice: Technical but accessible. Explain like a senior engineer briefing a sharp junior. No fear-mongering, no clickbait panic. Show the attack, explain why it works, give the mitigation. Respect the audience's intelligence.

topics.hook: Every AI you trust is whispering to strangers.
topics.angle: Prompt injection attacks on LLM-powered agents
topics.depth: What prompt injection is, 3 real attack vectors (indirect injection via web content, data exfiltration via tool calls, instruction override via context), and how to mitigate with input sanitization and output filtering.

topics.hook: Your API keys are in your git history right now.
topics.depth: How secrets end up in repos (env files, config commits, copy-paste), tools that scan for them (truffleHog, gitleaks, GitHub secret scanning), and the cleanup process (rotate, not just delete).

topics.hook: One employee clicked a link. The whole company went dark.
topics.angle: Anatomy of a modern ransomware attack
topics.depth: Initial access (phishing), lateral movement (credential harvesting, RDP), exfiltration before encryption, double extortion model. End with the 3-2-1 backup rule and network segmentation.

topics.hook: Your password is not your weakest link. Your phone number is.
topics.depth: How SIM swaps work (social engineering carrier support), why SMS-based 2FA is broken, hardware keys vs authenticator apps, and why your phone number is a public key to your identity.

topics.hook: The WiFi at your hotel is a confession booth.
topics.angle: Evil twin attacks and public network risks
topics.depth: How rogue access points work, SSL stripping, credential harvesting on captive portals. Mitigation: VPN, HTTPS-only mode, forget networks after use.

topics.hook: They didn't hack the company. They hacked the vendor.
topics.depth: SolarWinds, Codecov, Log4Shell pattern. How trusted dependencies become attack vectors. SBOM (Software Bill of Materials), dependency pinning, vendor security assessments.

topics.hook: Your browser extensions can see everything you type.
topics.angle: Browser extension permissions and data harvesting
topics.depth: What 'read and change all data on all websites' actually means, how extensions exfiltrate form data, and the review process that misses malicious updates. Audit your extensions.

topics.hook: The most dangerous hacker on earth uses a spreadsheet.
topics.depth: Pretexting, authority bias, urgency triggers, CEO fraud / BEC. Why technical controls fail against human psychology. Training and verification culture as the real defense.

topics.hook: Your smart home is a surveillance network you built yourself.
topics.angle: IoT device security and home network exposure
topics.depth: Default credentials on cameras/routers, UPnP exposure, firmware update gaps, network segmentation for IoT. The Mirai botnet as a case study.

topics.hook: Zero-day doesn't mean what you think it means.
topics.angle: Vulnerability lifecycle: from discovery to exploitation
topics.depth: What zero-day actually means (no patch available), CVE process, responsible disclosure vs drop, patch gap exploitation. Why 'just patch' is not a strategy.

topics.hook: DNS is the phonebook of the internet. And it's lying to you.
topics.depth: How DNS resolution works, cache poisoning attacks, DNS hijacking via compromised routers, BGP hijacks. DNSSEC, DoH, DoT as mitigations.

topics.hook: Your cloud is someone else's computer. And they left the door open.
topics.depth: Public S3 buckets, exposed Kubernetes dashboards, overprivileged IAM roles. Capital One breach as case study. CSPM tools, least privilege, infrastructure as code.

topics.hook: Encryption doesn't protect you if they already have the key.
topics.depth: Hardcoded keys, key rotation neglect, symmetric vs asymmetric misuse, HSMs and key vaults. Why encryption is only as strong as your key lifecycle.

topics.hook: The breach happened 6 months ago. You just don't know yet.
topics.depth: Average dwell time statistics, why attackers persist quietly, lateral movement and data staging. SIEM, EDR, threat hunting, and the importance of logging.

topics.hook: Your Docker container is running as root. That's the whole problem.
topics.depth: Root in containers, image vulnerabilities, runtime escapes. Distroless images, read-only filesystems, security contexts. The principle of least privilege applied to containers.

topics.hook: They cloned your voice with 3 seconds of audio.
topics.angle: AI-powered social engineering: deepfake voice and video
topics.depth: Voice cloning tech maturity, real cases of CEO voice fraud, video deepfakes in real-time calls. Verification protocols, callback procedures, codewords.

topics.hook: Your login page is the front door. CORS is the window you left open.
topics.depth: What same-origin policy protects, how misconfigured CORS headers expose APIs, CSRF token bypass, stored vs reflected XSS. Content Security Policy as defense.

topics.hook: The backup worked. The restore didn't.
topics.angle: Backup verification and disaster recovery testing
topics.depth: Untested backups are not backups. Corruption, encryption key loss, restore time assumptions. Tabletop exercises, automated restore testing, RPO vs RTO.

topics.hook: Rate limiting is not security. It's a speed bump.
topics.depth: Broken object-level authorization (BOLA), mass assignment, excessive data exposure. OWASP API Top 10. Schema validation, field-level authorization, API gateways.

topics.hook: Your CI/CD pipeline is the most powerful machine in your company. And nobody's watching it.
topics.depth: Pipeline poisoning, secret exposure in build logs, dependency confusion in package managers, compromised GitHub Actions. Pipeline hardening, OIDC tokens, signed artifacts.

topics.hook: The firewall is a castle wall. The attacker is already inside.
topics.depth: Why perimeter security fails, lateral movement after initial access. Zero trust principles: verify explicitly, least privilege, assume breach. BeyondCorp model, microsegmentation.

topics.hook: You gave the AI agent a tool. It used it against you.
topics.angle: Tool use abuse in AI agent systems
topics.depth: When agents misuse file system access, web browsing, code execution. Confused deputy attacks on agents. Sandboxing, capability restrictions, human-in-the-loop gates.

topics.hook: JWT tokens are not encrypted. They're base64 encoded. There's a difference.
topics.depth: JWTs are signed not encrypted, algorithm confusion attacks (none, HS256/RS256 swap), token expiry neglect, refresh token rotation. Proper JWT validation.

topics.hook: The compliance checkbox is green. The infrastructure is on fire.
topics.depth: Why passing SOC 2/ISO 27001 doesn't mean you're secure. Checkbox culture, audit theater, gap between policy and practice. Continuous validation over point-in-time audits.

topics.hook: Open source saved your project. Open source can also end it.
topics.depth: Typosquatting, maintainer account takeover, protestware (colors.js, node-ipc). Lockfiles, dependency review, Sigstore signing, reproducible builds.

topics.hook: Phishing emails don't look like phishing emails anymore.
topics.depth: LLMs generating perfect grammar, personalized context from LinkedIn/social, spear phishing at scale. Why traditional 'look for typos' advice is dead. Behavioral analysis, link sandboxing.

topics.hook: Your database query is running code you didn't write.
topics.depth: How SQLi still exists (string concatenation, ORMs bypassed), second-order injection, blind SQLi. Parameterized queries, WAFs, the principle that user input is never trusted.

topics.hook: The VPN protects your traffic. It doesn't protect your endpoints.
topics.depth: VPN tunnel vision — encrypted traffic but compromised devices. EDR, device posture checks, conditional access. Why VPN alone is insufficient for remote work security.

topics.hook: Logging everything is not monitoring. It's hoarding.
topics.depth: Alert fatigue, log volume vs signal quality, SIEM tuning. Detection engineering, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, actionable runbooks over raw alerts.

topics.hook: The attacker didn't bypass your auth. They stole the session cookie.
topics.depth: Session fixation, cookie theft via XSS, pass-the-cookie attacks. HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite flags, short-lived sessions, device binding.
